title: Long QT Drugs
body_html:
It is important to be aware of commonly used drugs that may cause QT prolongation. As QT prolongation may lead to potentially fatal arrhythmias such as Torsades de Pointes.

The list below summarises the more commonly used drugs that cause QT prolongation. A more exhaustive list can be found in the links.

Antimicrobials
Erythromycin
Clarithromycin
Ciproflaxacin
Moxifloxacin
Fluconazole
Ketoconazole

Antiarrhythmics
Procainamide
Sotalol
Quinidine
Amiodarone
Flecainide

Antipsychotics (all have some risk)
Risperidone
Haloperidol
Chlorpromazine
Quetiapine
Clozapine

Antidepressants
Citalopram
Fluoxetine
Amitriptyline
Clomipramine
Dosulepin
Doxepin
Imipramine
Lofepramine

Antiemetics
Domperidone
Droperidol
Ondansetron
Granisetron

Others
Methadone
Hydroxychloroquine
Chloroquine
Quinine
Some antiretrovirals
Loratadine
Crediblemeds.org21
Drugs causing prolonged QT
